original_abstract: A practical and atom-economical protocol for the synthesis of highly valuable benzoxepine skeletons has been developed via the formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition of readily available o-vinylphenols and alkynes. Catalyzed by a combination of [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2, the transformation proceeds through the selective cleavage of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl moiety. This versatile platform can be further extended by substituting the alkyne with carbon monoxide, facilitating a formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition that yields substituted coumarin derivatives.
humanized_abstract: A new, highly efficient and atom-economical route to benzoxepine derivatives of considerable importance, achieved by the formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition of o-vinylphenols and alkynes, is described. Employing a catalytic mixture of the rhodium compound [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2 in the reaction between the substrate and alkyne allows for selective activation of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl part of the substrate. Additionally, by using carbon monoxide instead of an alkyne in this reaction, formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition to the coumarin derivatives with various substituents is also achieved.

Additionally, by using carbon monoxide instead of an alkyne in this reaction, formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition to the coumarin derivatives with various substituents is also achieved.
